choices.C: I catered several meals in the past. PART 3 [32-34] WHello, Mr. Sanders. This is Julie Maple calling. I contacted you by e-mail this morning about a condo l'd like to look at. Do you have time to show me the place today? M I sure do, Ms. Maple. You mentioned you'd prefer to check it out around 3:30. Is that time still satisfactory for you? W Oh, no, it isn't. I've got a meeting scheduled at 4:00, so I'd like to see the place around 2:00 instead. That should give me plenty of time to check out the property. M Fortunately, I've got time then, so I can comply with your request. Why don't we meet at 52 Jackson Avenue at that time? [35-37] M I'm pleased we finally completed the interviews with the job candidates. I had no idea they were going to take nearly the entire day. W I know what you mean. I was on the intern search committee last year, and the interviews only required about half the time that this year's did. So which of the applicants did you like the most? I thought Sarah Carpenter was impressive and believe we should strongly consider her for a position. M She's definitely one of the leading candidates in my mind. We'll have to be sure to invite her back to conduct a second interview. [38-40] M Lara, how is the work on the redesign of the Fargo Building progressing? Do you think that you're going to be able to finish everything by tomorrow's deadline? W I'm afraid not, Mr. Ito. The work is taking much longer than I expected it to. However, I've started making good progress, so I am positive that I can submit the blueprints to you no later than noon on Friday. M All right. I'll call the client now and request an extension. But you had better finish everything by the new due date. The client is eager to get a look at the plans, and I don't want to disappoint him. [41-43] M Did you happen to read the news about promotions on the bulletin board? The list was just posted a few minutes ago. I can't believe Alice Milton got promoted to director. WYou've got to be kidding me. I heard she was probably going to get transferred to another branch or maybe even fired. Do you mean she's going to be our boss now? 4

choices.B: It will last for the entire month of May.
choices.C: It requires shoppers to make a purchase.
choices.D: It is only being offered to members.

questions.text: What is suggested on the invoice?

choices.A: Mr. Simpson can exchange the items that he bought.
choices.B: The toaster was purchased at a discounted price.
choices.C: A shipping fee was charged to deliver the items.
choices.D: The repairs on the video game console were worth less than $50.

questions.text: Why did Mr. Simpson write the letter?

choices.A: To ask about getting an item he owns repaired
choices.B: To express his pleasure with the service he received
choices.C: To find out how his video game machine was fixed
choices.D: To complain about the quality of some repairs

questions.text: What does Mr. Simpson indicate about Al's Electronics?

choices.A: It will have another special sale soon.
choices.B: It employs Jason Hampton as a manager.
choices.C: It was unable to repair his tape recorder.
choices.D: It overcharged him for an item that he bought.

questions.text: For whom is the announcement most likely intended?
What is NOT mentioned about Mr. Brown?

choices.A: The employees at the bank like him.
choices.B: He has worked at the bank for four decades.
choices.C: He founded the branch in St. Louis.
choices.D: The bank has done well since he became vice president.

questions.text: According to the memo, when will the retirement party be held?
Why did Ms. Walters write the e-mail?

choices.B: To offer a discount on an ordered item
choices.C: To discuss a problem with an item
choices.D: To note that some items have been shipped

questions.text: What is suggested about the employees at the bank?

choices.A: They donated $3,500 for Mr. Brown's gift.
choices.B: They will all attend Mr. Brown's retirement party.
choices.C: They suggested what to buy to Ms. Rudolph.
choices.D: They are eager to meet their new vice president.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the instructions?

choices.A: To describe how to repair a product
choices.B: To show how to assemble the product
choices.C: To demonstrate how to take apart a product
choices.D: To explain how to use a product

questions.text: According to the first e-mail, how did Ms. Russell use the bowl improperly?

choices.A: She turned the handle the wrong way.
choices.B: She washed the bowl in her dishwasher.
choices.C: She added vegetables that were not cut.
choices.D: She failed to clean the bowl properly.

questions.text: What does Ms. Russell send along with her e-mail?

choices.D: A copy of the instruction manual Stop! This is the end of the test. If you finish before time is called, you may go back to Parts 5, 6, and 7 and check your work.

questions.text: Why does Mr. Martin refuse to refund Ms. Russell's money?

choices.A: She did not buy the item from Sussex's website.
choices.B: Her use of the bowl voided the warranty.
choices.C: Sussex has a policy banning giving refunds.
choices.D: She bought the item more than one month ago.

questions.text: What does Mr. Martin suggest that Ms. Russell do?

choices.A: Call him if she has any future problems
choices.B: Use a coupon to purchase another item
choices.C: Visit the store and request a refund
choices.D: Attempt to repair the bowl herself 73
